sglang.author: xqoasis

Motivation


Fix #3901
The execute notebook CI is to test the correctness of PRs. If one CI is merged into main, it should not be triggered, but just use execute-and-deploy.

Drop the line that triggers execute notebook CI on Push action

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Add test example for Ultravox v0.5
vllm.author: DarkLight1337

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Handle None parameters in Mistral function calls.
vllm.author: fgreinacher
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a follow-up of #12884 which was missing the case when a client explicitly passes None function parameters. While not super useful this is technically valid and should also be allowed.
⚒️ with ❤️ by @siemens

vllm.title: [Misc]: Add support for goodput on guided benchmarking + TPOT calculation refactor
vllm.author: b8zhong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Implement Goodput Calculation + Fix TPOT Calc in Benchmark
This PR touches benchmark_serving_guided.py by adding goodput calculation and correcting TPOT assignment.
Changes:


Added goodput calculation:

Introduced the --goodput argument (consistent with benchmark_serving.py), accepting SLOs as "KEY

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Do not crash V0 engine on input errors
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR adds exception handling to the V0 engine that catches errors processing the input for a sequence group, and removes that sequence group from the current batch instead of crashing the entire engine. We see this a lot when processing invalid image data for multimodal models, the mllama models

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Update expected token counts for Ultravox tests
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR fixes another problem introduced by the updated HF processor for Ultravox v0.5. For more context, see #13871 a mismatch in token counts introduced by #13871

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Backend option to disable xgrammar any_whitespace
vllm.author: wallashss
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Mistral models + guided decoding with json schema with xgrammar is generating endless whitespace.  This bug was introduced with this change on xgrammar. My proposal is to add an environment variable that can disable whitespace on guided decoding with json scheme. Therefore, serving models like mistr

vllm.title: [BugFix] Make FP8 Linear compatible with torch.compile
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR registers the FP8 block linear op for torch.compile

vllm.title: [Kernel] FlashMLA integration
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Integrate: https://github.com/deepseek-ai/FlashMLA
currently requires
export VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASHMLA

and
block_size=64

TODO:

cuda-graphs are broken
future PR: enforce block_size 64 gracefully

Closes #13735

vllm.title: [ROCm][Quantization][Kernel] Use FP8 FNUZ when OCP flag is 0 or undefined
vllm.author: HollowMan6
vllm.bodyExcerpt: In older versions of "hip/hip_fp8.h" header (before ROCm/clr@353f15a), both HIP_FP8_TYPE_OCP and HIP_FP8_TYPE_FNUZ are not defined, and the code implementation is in FNUZ, so it's better to write it in this way, such that we don't check if HIP_FP8_TYPE_FNUZ is there or not, and we just use FP8 FNUZ

vllm.title: Use CUDA 12.4 as default for release and nightly wheels
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: I think it is time to match pytorch's default cuda version. We should still keep wheels built with 12.1 around.

vllm.title: [misc] Rename Ray ADAG to Compiled Graph
vllm.author: ruisearch42
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Ray ADAG was officially renamed to Ray Compiled Graph in the end of 2024.
This PR cleans up the code to use the new terminology. No logic change.

vllm.title: [ROCm][V1] Update reshape_and_cache to properly work with CUDA graph padding
vllm.author: SageMoore
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This patch updates reshape_and_cache to account for the case where slot_mapping.size(0) is not equal to key.size(0). This occurs when we use CUDA graph padding on V1.
Fixes: #13418

vllm.title: [V1][Metrics] Handle preemptions
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Part of #10582
Add a core engine PREEMPTED event.
Add the num_preemptions_total counter from v0.
Also, make preemptions reset the scheduled and first token timestamps resulting in:
  << queued timestamp >>
    [ queue interval ]
  << scheduled timestamp (FIRST) >>
    [ prefill interval ]
  << new t
